aronui.when_to_use: To understand the structure of a code file (classes, functions, methods with line numbers) before reading large files, to know which sections to request with start_line/end_line.
aronui.output_shape: A list of classes, functions, and methods with their line numbers.

pitfalls.text: Missing required parameters (owner, repo, path) may cause validation errors, but exact error texts were not observable due to an unmet prerequisite. All attempts returned the same error: 'GitHub is no
The tool requires a connected GitHub account; otherwise it will only return the connection error regardless of other parameter validity.

procedure: Ensure your GitHub account is connected in Settings → Connections → Developer → GitHub.
Provide the required parameters: owner (repository owner), repo (repository name), path (file path relative to repo root). Optionally provide ref (branch/tag/commit).
verification: Check that the output is a list of classes, functions, and methods with line numbers, indicating successful parsing.
If the output contains 'GitHub is not connected', connect your GitHub account and try again.

predict_records.predicted: success: The tool will parse the Python file and return a list of classes and functions with their line numbers.
predict_records.actual: success: GitHub is not connected. Connect your GitHub account in Settings → Connections → Developer → GitHub. Then the agent can list your repos, read files, and list issues/PRs.
